verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (human kallikrein-2) is a secreted serine protease expressed predominantly in prostate epithelium. It is synthesized as a preproenzyme, processed through the secretory pathway, and released extracellularly as a soluble enzyme. It has no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no outer-leaflet lipidation. Checking all 5 contextual buckets: (1) no documented cancer ecto-pool on the outer leaflet as a surface-anchored form — serum PSA/KLK2 assays detect circulating soluble protein; (2) no documented cell-death-induced PM translocation; (3) expression is tissue-restricted to prostate but the secreted form is not stably anchored to a TM partner — it diffuses into ductal fluid and bloodstream; (4) no documented PM cycling minority pool; (5) no wash-resistant covalent tethering to a TM scaffold. Surface-directed therapeutic programs (e.g., prodrug activation strategies) exploit its secreted enzymatic activity, not a cell-surface-anchored form. Verdict: secreted_only.
